prompt: I have a scaffolded Go package at ./kvrx/ with tokenizer.go/parser.go/printer.go stubs and a SPEC.md describing the KVRX format (a richer extension of KVR). Implement support for boolean record values AND the `if`/`elif`/`else` conditional statement end-to-end (per SPEC.md's Lexical Elements, Structure (Grammar), and Semantics sections). Specifically: tokenize the keywords `true`, `false`, `if`, `elif`, `else` (still as TokenIdentifier per spec; the parser distinguishes by Value), parse `record bool KEY = true|false`, parse the Conditional statement with its `if (Expression) { ... }` body and any `elif`/`else` clauses, and add a Bool literal AST node and a Conditional AST node. The Conditional's expression must be evaluated at parse time using previously-declared records (a tiny scope-walking helper is enough — full Expression resolution can be a stub that handles `&NAME` lookups and `==` comparison against bool literals). I want tokenizer tests with exact Pos values, parser tests that drive Parse() (no hand-constructed AST), a direct printer test, and a round-trip test for both record-bool and the conditional statement. Don't read the whole SPEC.md — slice it. Note: the parser-phase slices for KVRX clear the 600-line gate, so the orchestrator must partition the parser (and likely printer) phase into sub-units before launching subagents — announce the plan up front and write partition_plan.md.

assertions.text: parser.go defines a Bool literal AST node (e.g. BoolLiteral struct or named constant) with a Value field
parser.go defines a Conditional struct (or equivalent AST node) with at least an If branch (condition + body), an Elifs slice, and an Else body
parser.go has a parser path that handles `record bool KEY = true` / `... = false` and stores the bool value on the resulting AST node
parser.go has a parseConditional (or equivalent named action) that recognises `if`/`elif`/`else` keyword identifiers and builds the Conditional AST
parser.go evaluates the conditional's expression at parse time against previously-declared record values (a scope-walking lookup function or environment is present)
parser.go uses p.expect(...) at sites that require a specific token type — no inline TokenType comparisons in the new code
printer.go has a function or action that emits a Conditional back to source text (with `if (...) { ... }`, optional `elif (...) { ... }`, optional `else { ... }`)
printer.go emits `true` or `false` as the value of a bool record
tokenizer_test.go has a test for one of the new keywords (true/false/if) that asserts an exact Pos{Line, Column} value
parser_test.go uses Parse(strings.NewReader(...)) for the test inputs and does not hand-construct Conditional or Bool literals as expected values (the empty-input scaffold case excepted)
parser_test.go has a subtest that parses a `record bool ENABLED = true; if (&ENABLED) { record number PORT = 443; }`-shaped input and asserts the Conditional AST
printer_test.go has a direct test that calls Print on a *File containing a Conditional and asserts the output string matches an expected literal
printer_test.go has a round-trip test (Parse → Print → Parse → require.Equal) covering both record-bool and the conditional statement
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
tests use github.com/stretchr/testify/require
go test -race ./... exits cleanly
no leftover _spec_*.md or _context_*.md scratch files in the package directory (partition_plan.md is preserved per SKILL.md and is NOT a scratch file)
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content was written to the package (e.g. _spec_tokens.md should not exist)
partition_plan.md exists in the package directory after the run (per the SKILL.md ## Outputs and ## Cleanup instructions)
partition_plan.md reports that at least one phase was partitioned into 2+ sub-units (kvrx's parser-phase slices total over 600 lines, so the gate must trip)

partition_plan.md's per-sub-unit start/done log shows non-overlapping timestamps within each partitioned phase (sub-unit i's `done` precedes sub-unit i+1's `starting`) — proof that sub-calls ran serially, not in parallel

SKILL.md specifies, for both _context_tokens.md and _context_ast.md, a strict signature-only format (no rationale, no examples), a hard 400-line cap, and a chunk-and-relaunch protocol when the cap would be exceeded (issue #46 acceptance)

SKILL.md specifies the issue #47 phase-chunking protocol: an up-front scope gate in `Before you start` (numeric threshold, partitioning into sub-units along section boundaries, plan announced to the user before any subagent launches) and a `## Phase chunking` section saying sub-calls run serially, append to the running _context_tokens.md / _context_ast.md, and do not full-read the growing tokenizer.go / parser.go / printer.go source file (issue #47 acceptance)
